name: Kyiv
terrain_description: Split by Dnipro River; western side features Irpin River obstacle with limited crossing points; eastern side open with high-speed routes; agricultural outskirts with wooded areas; dam at hydroelectric plant creates reservoir to north.

name: Kharkiv
terrain_description: Located in flat terrain near Russian border; large urban sprawl with Soviet-era industrial districts; extensive grid network facilitates urban combat; proximity to border made it initial invasion target.

name: Odesa
terrain_description: Coastal city on Black Sea; catacombs network underground; port facilities and industrial areas; accessible primarily by land from west due to sea and estuary barriers.

name: Dnipro
terrain_description: Spans both banks of Dnipro River; major industrial center with river port; strategic crossing point; hilly right bank overlooks left bank.

name: Donetsk
terrain_description: Located on high ground in Donbas; 'fortress belt' terrain favors defender with elevation advantage; observation and artillery coordination superior from city heights; downhill westward approach.

name: Lviv
terrain_description: Western Ukraine foothills; historic compact center with narrow streets; surrounded by wooded hills; rail hub; relatively untouched by ground combat.

name: Zaporizhzhia
terrain_description: On Dnipro River adjacent to Russian-controlled nuclear plant; industrial terrain with extensive factory complexes; Dnipro hydroelectric station creates crossing obstacle.

name: Kryvyi Rih
terrain_description: Extremely elongated city stretching 126 km along iron ore deposits; industrial terrain with mines and tailings ponds; multiple water obstacles from Inhulets River.

name: Sevastopol
terrain_description: Crimean port on multiple bays; mountainous peninsula terrain; naval base with fortified harbor; occupied since 2014.

name: Mykolaiv
terrain_description: At confluence of Southern Buh and Inhul rivers; shipbuilding center; flat terrain with river obstacles; strategic crossing point toward Odesa.

name: Mariupol
terrain_description: Coastal city on Sea of Azov; Azovstal and Ilyich steel plants provided fortress-like defensive positions; extensive industrial underground structures; completely destroyed during siege.

name: Luhansk
terrain_description: Donbas region near Russian border; rolling hills with coal mining terrain; industrial urban landscape; occupied since 2014.

name: Vinnytsia
terrain_description: Southern Buh River valley; surrounded by agricultural land; airbase location; relatively central location away from front lines.

name: Simferopol
terrain_description: Crimean interior at Salhir River; surrounded by Crimean Mountains foothills; transport hub; occupied since 2014.

name: Makiivka
terrain_description: Donetsk suburban area/eastern contiguous urban zone; industrial terrain similar to Donetsk; coal mining infrastructure; occupied since 2014.

name: Chernihiv
terrain_description: Desna River valley near Belarus border; surrounded by forests and wetlands; river obstacles limited Russian armored approaches; besieged early in war.

name: Poltava
terrain_description: Vorskla River bank; elevated terrain with historic center on hill; agricultural surroundings; key logistics hub.

name: Kherson
terrain_description: Dnipro River mouth on Black Sea; flat terrain with canals; opposite Russian-held east bank; contested crossing point; liberated November 2022.

name: Khmelnytskyi
terrain_description: Southern Buh River; Podillia upland terrain; rail junction; western region away from front.

name: Cherkasy
terrain_description: Dnipro River reservoir bank; steep right bank overlooks left bank; industrial and transport hub.

name: Chernivtsi
terrain_description: Carpathian foothills on Prut River; hilly terrain with mix of architectural styles; near Romanian border; safe from ground combat.

name: Zhytomyr
terrain_description: Teteriv River valley; wooded area with granitic outcroppings; key transport node west of Kyiv; early war Russian advance reached outskirts.

name: Sumy
terrain_description: Psel River near Russian border; flat agricultural terrain; surrounded by farmland; besieged early in war.

name: Rivne
terrain_description: Ustia River valley; Polesian lowland with forests; northwestern Ukraine; transport hub away from front.

name: Horlivka
terrain_description: Donbas coal mining terrain; hilly industrial landscape with mine shafts; occupied since 2014.

name: Ivano-Frankivsk
terrain_description: Carpathian foothills on Bystrytsia River; hilly terrain; western Ukraine cultural center; unaffected by ground combat.

name: Kamianske
terrain_description: Dnipro River bank; industrial city with large steel plant; flat terrain with river obstacle.

name: Ternopil
terrain_description: Seret River valley; pond-dotted terrain; historic center on island; western region away from front.

name: Kropyvnytskyi
terrain_description: Inhul River; central Ukrainian upland; agricultural surroundings; logistics hub.

name: Lutsk
terrain_description: Styr River; Volynian upland; historic castle; northwestern Ukraine near Polish border.

name: Kremenchuk
terrain_description: Dnipro River bank; major industrial center with oil refinery and rail car plant; flat terrain; strategic river crossing.

name: Bila Tserkva
terrain_description: Ros River; agricultural terrain south of Kyiv; large military base; early war staging area.

name: Kerch
terrain_description: Strait of Kerch between Crimea and Russia; hilly terrain at sea junction; Crimean Bridge connects to Russia; occupied since 2014.

name: Melitopol
terrain_description: Molochna River in steppe zone; flat agricultural terrain; key transport node; occupied since 2022.

name: Kramatorsk
terrain_description: Donbas region; hilly industrial terrain with machine building plants; Ukrainian administrative center for Donetsk Oblast; frequent shelling target.

name: Uzhhorod
terrain_description: Carpathian Mountains foothills on Uzh River; near Slovak border; lowland valley surrounded by hills; safe from combat.

name: Brovary
terrain_description: Kyiv eastern suburb; flat terrain on highway M01 approach to capital; site of major tank battle March 2022.

name: Yevpatoriia
terrain_description: Crimean west coast on Black Sea; flat steppe coastal terrain; resort city; occupied since 2014.

name: Berdiansk
terrain_description: Azov Sea coast at Berda River mouth; flat coastal terrain with port; occupied since 2022.

name: Alchevsk
terrain_description: Donbas rolling hills; heavy industrial city with steel and metallurgy; occupied since 2014.

name: Nikopol
terrain_description: Dnipro River bank opposite Russian-held Enerhodar; manganese mining center; flat terrain; frequent shelling across river.

name: Sloviansk
terrain_description: Donbas region with salt lakes; hilly terrain; strategic high ground; site of 2014 battle and 2022 frontline.

name: Pavlohrad
terrain_description: Steppe zone on Vovcha River; industrial and rail hub; flat terrain; strategic reserve area.

name: Sievierodonetsk
terrain_description: Donbas on Siverskyi Donets River; chemical industry city; flat terrain with river obstacle; completely destroyed during 2022 battle.

name: Kamianets-Podilskyi
terrain_description: Canyon of Smotrych River; castle on peninsula formed by river bend; rugged terrain with deep canyon; historic fortress city.

name: Lysychansk
terrain_description: Donbas on Siverskyi Donets River high right bank; elevated terrain overlooks Sievierodonetsk; industrial and refinery center; fell June 2022.

name: Mukacheve
terrain_description: Carpathian foothills on Latorica River; castle on volcanic hill; near Hungarian border; safe from combat.

name: Konotop
terrain_description: Sumy Oblast near Russian border; flat agricultural terrain with river; early war Russian advance route.

name: Uman
terrain_description: Cherkasy Oblast on Umanka River; hilly terrain with park; major Hasidic pilgrimage site; Hasidic pilgrimage site; key road junction.

name: Khrustalnyi
terrain_description: Donbas mining terrain; hilly industrial landscape; occupied since 2014.

name: Yenakiieve
terrain_description: Donbas industrial terrain with metallurgy; hilly mining landscape; occupied since 2014.

name: Oleksandriia
terrain_description: Kirovohrad Oblast on Inhulets River; flat terrain with lignite mining; agricultural surroundings.

name: Yalta
terrain_description: Crimean south coast; mountainous coastal terrain with amphitheater layout; resort city; occupied since 2014.

name: Drohobych
terrain_description: Lviv Oblast in foothills; historic salt mining town; hilly terrain; western Ukraine away from front.

name: Kadiivka
terrain_description: Donbas mining terrain; coal industry; occupied since 2014.

name: Berdychiv
terrain_description: Zhytomyr Oblast on Hnylopiat River; rolling terrain; historic city; rail junction.

name: Shostka
terrain_description: Sumy Oblast near Russian border; flat terrain with chemical industry; early war Russian incursion area.

name: Bakhmut
terrain_description: Donetsk Oblast on Bakhmutka River; hilly industrial terrain with salt mines; site of longest and bloodiest battle of war; completely destroyed.

name: Izmail
terrain_description: Danube River delta near Romanian border; port city on Chilia branch; flat terrain with fortress; UNESCO Biosphere Reserve nearby.

name: Samar
terrain_description: Dnipropetrovsk Oblast at Samara River confluence; flat terrain; industrial and rail center.

name: Kovel
terrain_description: Volyn Oblast on Turiia River; flat Polesian lowland; key rail hub near Polish border; safe from combat.

name: Feodosiia
terrain_description: Crimean southeast coast; coastal mountain backdrop; port city with historic fortifications; occupied since 2014.

name: Kostiantynivka
terrain_description: Donetsk Oblast on Kryvyi Torets River; industrial city with glass and metallurgy; hilly terrain; frequent frontline shelling.

name: Nizhyn
terrain_description: Chernihiv Oblast on Oster River; flat terrain with historic architecture; early war Russian advance route.

name: Smila
terrain_description: Cherkasy Oblast on Tiasmyn River; flat terrain; rail junction; industrial center.

name: Irpin
terrain_description: Kyiv suburb northwest of capital; Irpin River obstacle with limited bridges; wooded area; site of intense urban combat during 2022 Battle of Kyiv; partially destroyed.

name: Kalush
terrain_description: Ivano-Frankivsk Oblast on Dniester tributary; Carpathian foothills; chemical industry; western Ukraine.

name: Sheptytskyi
terrain_description: Lviv Oblast near Polish border; hilly terrain; coal mining town; western region.

name: Boryspil
terrain_description: Kyiv Oblast east of capital; flat terrain; site of Ukraine's main international airport; key logistics hub.

name: Dovzhansk
terrain_description: Luhansk Oblast near Russian border; hilly mining terrain; occupied since 2014.

name: Pervomaisk
terrain_description: Mykolaiv Oblast on Southern Buh River; flat terrain with river crossing; agricultural region.

name: Korosten
terrain_description: Zhytomyr Oblast on Uzh River; wooded Polesian terrain; rail junction; early war Russian advance toward Kyiv.

name: Kolomyia
terrain_description: Ivano-Frankivsk Oblast on Prut River; Carpathian foothills; Hutsul culture center; safe from combat.

name: Pokrovsk
terrain_description: Donetsk Oblast; hilly industrial terrain; key logistics hub for Ukrainian forces; frequent Russian attacks; critical defensive position.

name: Stryi
terrain_description: Lviv Oblast on Stryi River; Carpathian foothills; important rail junction; western Ukraine.

name: Chornomorsk
terrain_description: Odesa Oblast southwest of Odesa; Black Sea coast; port city with flat coastal terrain; ferry terminal to Georgia and Turkey.

name: Khartsyzk
terrain_description: Donetsk Oblast; hilly industrial terrain; occupied since 2014.
